title: CachingStore: lock never held across await — documented and enforced by design
description: The tokio::sync::Mutex is released in a block scope before every inner.get() / inner.put() / inner.refresh() call. This is explicitly documented in the crate-level doc comment. A future contributor might move the await inside the lock scope thinking it simplifies the code. Do not. Holding an async mutex across an await point is a deadlock waiting to happen — any other task calling get() on the same CachingStore while the lock is held will block the executor thread forever (current_thread runtime) or serialize all cache users (multi-thread runtime). The thundering-herd issue (9sn.1) is the price of this correct design; pay it.
